title: Fix a data-pipeline bug in the transform, not the output
task: `npm test` fails: the parse -> normalize -> render pipeline produces the wrong discounted price. The symptom shows up in the rendered string, but src/parse.js (input) and src/render.js (output) are correct — the bug is in the discount math in src/normalize.js (the transform). Fix the transform so a price discounted by `rate` (e.g. 0.1 = 10% off) is computed correctly. Do not change src/render.js or src/parse.js, and do not modify the tests. Run the tests to verify.
notes: The cause is in the transform (multiplying by rate instead of 1 - rate); file_not_contains proves the transform was fixed rather than the renderer compensating. The render pin confirms the output stage was left intact.
